about.title: Fondazzjoni — ESRF.net
about.description: Dwar ESRF.net — il-Qafas Ewropew dwar is-Sigurtà u r-Reżiljenza, il-karta tiegħu, il-ħames pilastri morali.
about.hero_deck: Il-Qafas Ewropew dwar is-Sigurtà u r-Reżiljenza — il-pedament ta' ESRF.net — huwa mibni fuq konvinzjoni sempliċi.
about.charter_quote: Is-sigurtà mhijiex biss mandat. Hija responsabbiltà li nġorru flimkien.
about.what_sub: Deċentralizzat fl-istruttura, ċiviku fl-għan, Ewropew fil-firxa.
about.what_body_1_html: ESRF.net huwa l-livell tal-komunità tal-Qafas Ewropew dwar is-Sigurtà & r-Reżiljenza.
about.what_body_2_html: Fejn il-gvernijiet jistabbilixxu l-politika u l-korpi internazzjonali jikkoordinaw, ESRF.net tgħaqqad lill-intraprendituri, l-istituzzjonijiet u r-rispondituri tal-ewwel li l-prattika ta’ kuljum tagħhom hi s-sottostrat attwali tar-reżiljenza. Nikklassifikaw l-organizzazzjonijiet fi għaxar setturi vitali — allinjati mad-Direttiva NIS2 tal-UE.
about.what_pull_html: Aħna mhux regolatur. Aħna komunità ta' prattikanti.
about.what_body_3: Il-fondazzjoni tħarreġ id-donazzjonijiet, il-kontribuzzjonijiet u l-kapital allinjat f'inizjattivi li jsaħħu s-sigurtà u r-reżiljenza Ewropea.
about.pillar1_body_html: Tinduna qabel isir storbjuż. L-ewwel pilastru hu l-attenzjoni paċenzjuża u strutturali lejn sinjali, xenarji u riskji sistemiċi — intelliġenza li tasal jum bikri wisq u qatt minuta tard wisq.
about.pillar2_body_html: Tipproteġi dak li jsostnina. It-tieni pilastru hu d-dixxiplina li tiddefendi l-perimetri fiżiċi, diġitali u istituzzjonali. Il-kustodja tfisser li m’intix is-sid ta’ dak li tipproteġi; inti żżommu għal kull min jiddependi fuqu.
about.pillar3_body_html: Tkun lest, flimkien. It-tielet pilastru hu l-kultivazzjoni tal-opzjonijiet — il-kapaċità li taġixxi b’kunfidenza meta pjan jiltaqa’ mat-temp. Il-preparazzjoni mhix ħażna; hi l-kompetenza kondiviża li tadatta.
about.pillar4_body_html: Taġixxi bħala wieħed meta jkun importanti. Ir-raba’ pilastru hu l-virtù tal-wasla — b’affidabbiltà, malajr, flimkien. Is-solidarjetà mhix sentiment; hi prattika li trid tiġi mħarrġa ħafna qabel ma tkun meħtieġa.
about.pillar5_body_html: Tqum aktar b’saħħtek minn qabel. Il-ħames pilastru hu d-dixxiplina li tassorbi skossi u tirristartja s-sistemi. It-tiġdid jittratta kull rkupru bħala opportunità biex tissewwa dak li l-aħħar kriżi żvelat.
about.join_sub: Id-dħul fil-lista huwa bla ħlas. L-istwardija hija l-uniku tariffa li niġbru.
about.join_body_1: Kull organizzazzjoni — kummerċjali, istituzzjonali, mhux għall-qligħ — li l-ħidma tagħha ssaħħaħ is-sigurtà u r-reżiljenza Ewropea hija milqugħa.
about.join_step_1_html: Itlob dħul fil-lista. Imla l-formola tal-applikazzjoni bl-isem tal-organizzazzjoni tiegħek, deskrizzjoni ta’ paragrafu u s-settur prinċipali tal-prattika tiegħek.
about.join_step_2_html: Allinja mal-karta. Kull organizzazzjoni elencata tissieħeb mal-karta ta' ESRF.net — il-ħames prinċipji morali.
about.join_step_3_html: Idher fuq l-atlas. Il-lista tiegħek tidher fuq id-direttorju, il-mappa u — fejn rilevanti — l-analitika.
about.join_body_2: Kontribuzzjonijiet lill-fondazzjoni — finanzjarji jew in natura — huma milqugħa iżda qatt meħtieġa. ESRF.net ma jbiegħx lista u ma jaffarjax reklamar.
about.gov_entity_body: ESRF.net hu mħaddem mill-Stichting ESRF European Security and Resilience Fund, fondazzjoni mingħajr skop ta' qligħ irreġistrata fl-Olanda (KvK). Il-fondazzjoni — imsejħa ESRF.net meta taġixxi bħala faċilitatur tal-komunità — teżisti biss biex iżżomm il-qafas, tiffinanzja l-operazzjoni editorjali u tipproteġi l-indipendenza tad-direttorju.
about.gov_methodology_body_html: L-organizzazzjonijiet huma klassifikati f'għaxar setturi vitali allinjati mad-Direttiva tal-UE NIS2 u d-Direttiva dwar ir-Reżiljenza tal-Entitajiet Kritiċi (CER). Kull elenkar jiġi rivedut editorjalment skont tliet kriterji: (1) ix-xogħol tal-organizzazzjoni għandu jikkontribwixxi b'mod dimostrabbli għas-sigurtà jew ir-reżiljenza Ewropea, (2) għandha topera fi jew tservi tal-anqas waħda mill-{countries} pajjiżi koperti, u (3) għandu jkollha preżenza pubblika verifikabbli (sit web, reġistrazzjoni mal-kamra tal-kummerċ jew ekwivalenti).
about.gov_selection_body: ESRF.net ma jbigħx elenkar, ma jikkalassifikax lill-membri tiegħu u ma jitlobx ħlasijiet ta' abbonament. L-organizzazzjonijiet jistgħu jitolbu elenkar jew jiġu identifikati permezz ta' intelliġenza minn sorsi miftuħa. Kull elenkar jiġi rivedut mit-tim editorjali skont it-tassonomija settorjali u t-tliet kriterji ta' hawn fuq. Elenkar li ma jissodisfax aktar il-kriterji jiġi arkivjat, mhux imħassar — it-trasparenza tapplika fiż-żewġ direzzjonijiet.
about.gov_stichting_body: Il-fondazzjoni (Stichting ESRF European Security and Resilience Fund) hi l-entità legali. In-network hu l-komunità ta' {total} organizzazzjonijiet. Il-fondazzjoni ma tippossjedix, ma tikkontrollax u ma tirrappreżentax l-organizzazzjonijiet li telenka — hi tikkura d-direttorju, iżżomm l-atlas, tippubblika d-dispatch u tikkanalizza l-kontribuzzjonijiet f'inizjattivi li jkabbru u jsaħħu n-network. L-organizzazzjonijiet infushom huma sovrani; id-direttorju hu ġid pubbliku.
about.gov_contact_body_html:
Stichting ESRF European Security and Resilience Fund · l-Olanda
Mistoqsijiet ġenerali: hello@esrf.net
Kwistjonijiet ta' privatezza: privacy@esrf.net

sponsor.faq1_a: Le. ESRF.net huwa indipendenti editorjalment. L-isponsor jiġu rikonoxxuti b'mod trasparenti, iżda ma għandhom l-ebda influwenza fuq dak li nippubblikaw.
sponsor.faq2_a: Il-ftehimiet ta' sponsorja huma annwali. Jiġu offruti tiġdidiet lill-isponsor attwali l-ewwel, qabel ma jinfetħu postijiet ġodda.
sponsor.faq3_q: Nistgħu nisponsorjaw pajjiż jew settur speċifiku?
sponsor.faq3_a: Iva — l-isponsorjati Programme Partner jistgħu jiġu allinjati ma’ settur vitali speċifiku tal-għażla tiegħek. Jekk l-organizzazzjoni tiegħek topera, ngħidu aħna, fl-Infrastruttura Kritika jew fil-Manifattura tas-Saħħa u Medika, il-logo tiegħek se jidher fuq il-paġni ta’ dawk is-sezzjonijiet. Semmihom fit-talba tiegħek u naġġustaw il-ftehim kif xieraq.
sponsor.faq4_a: Naċċettaw trasferiment bankarju (SEPA u wajering internazzjonali) u fatturar. Id-dettalji sħaħ jiġu pprovduti wara l-konferma tal-mistoqsija.
sponsor.faq5_q: ESRF.net huwa miftuħ għal organizzazzjonijiet barra mill-Ewropa?
sponsor.faq5_a: Iva. Nilqgħu mistoqsijiet ta' sponsorja minn organizzazzjonijiet madwar id-dinja li l-ħidma tagħhom tinterseka mal-prijoritajiet tas-sigurtà u r-reżiljenza Ewropea.

editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html: Since the end of February 2026, the Strait of Hormuz has been blocked as a result of the armed conflict between the United States, Israel and Iran.[1] [2] This blockade affects the main transit route for oil from the Middle East and thus impacts the entire European energy supply.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_2: Shell CEO Wael Sawan warned on 24 March that Europe could expect physical shortages from April onwards — first with kerosene, then diesel and finally petrol.[3] [4] In March, global oil production fell by around 10.1 million barrels a day, one of the most severe supply shocks in decades. [5]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_3: On 11 March, the International Energy Agency (IEA) released a record 400 million barrels from strategic reserves.[7][6] In the Netherlands, phase 1 of the National Oil Crisis Plan (LCP-O) came into effect on 17 April: there are no acute shortages as yet, but the government is stepping up monitoring and consulting with major consumers in the transport sector.[8] [9] The European Commission does not currently anticipate an immediate shortage, but is preparing a package of measures.[10] [11]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.h2_2: What does this mean for Europe — and for businesses?
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_4: At European level, efforts are being focused on three areas: supply measures such as the release of strategic reserves and additional LNG imports; demand reduction through reduced travel and working from home; and phased contingency plans for each Member State.[9][12][6] Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen is preparing the EU for what he calls a “prolonged” energy crisis.[13] The Dutch LCP-O has four escalation phases, with the government focusing primarily on voluntary measures and communication up to and including the most severe phase. [8]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_5: For businesses, it boils down to this: reducing fuel consumption, combining journeys, taking a critical look at the vehicle fleet, spreading out deliveries, and exploring alternative routes and energy sources. But the most important thing is to communicate clearly and in good time with customers about what is changing and why.[14] [15]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.tip_title: Communicate early on — don’t wait until there’s a problem
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_6: In a post-pandemic market, customers interpret silence as bad news.[14] Keep them proactively informed as soon as you spot risks in your supply chain, even if you cannot yet provide a precise schedule.[15] Never[16] wait until a delivery fails.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_7: Don’t focus on your own internal issues. Explain exactly what the oil shortage means for their order, delivery time or price.[14]Keep it brief and outline the options available to the customer.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.tip_title_3: Be transparent about the causes and the uncertainties
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_8: Explain that the situation stems from a geopolitical shock — the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz — and that governments are also facing uncertainty.[1] [10] Customers are more likely to accept bad news if they understand the background and feel that you are being completely open with them.[16] [17]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_9: Use specific facts and figures: "delivery times will increase from 3 to 7 working days" or "fuel surcharge of X% from 1 May".[17]Phrases such as "possible slight delay" actually cause more anxiety than they provide clarity.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.tip_title_5: Back up price rises with facts
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_10: Many businesses are having to raise their prices due to increased fuel and input costs. Be honest about this rather than glossing over it.[16] Cite specific figures — such as kerosene prices having doubled or the diesel price index — so that customers can see that this is not an arbitrary price hike. [3]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_11: Give customers options: alternative products, bundled deliveries, flexible delivery dates, digital alternatives to in-person visits, or longer-term contracts that offer price certainty.[14] When people have[17] a choice, they feel less like victims.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_12: Identify where your target audience gets their information — newsletters, WhatsApp, LinkedIn, customer portals — and communicate consistently through those channels.[15]KFC handled its 2018 chicken crisis well by responding actively on social media and maintaining a live update of which branches were open. [18]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_13: Show that you understand the customer’s frustration. A phrase such as “I understand how important this delivery is to you” goes further than a discount.[17]Empathy builds trust in a way that promotions cannot.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.tip_title_9: Link your message to sustainability — but only if it makes sense
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_14: Operational changes such as reducing the number of journeys, driving more economically and electrifying the vehicle fleet often align with existing sustainability targets.[12] [13] Communicate this, but only if it is transparent and credible. Greenwashing is severely penalised.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_15: Develop contingency plans now for scenarios such as price rises, delivery delays and distribution disruptions. Use flexible templates, not rigid scripts.[16] [17] Train your customer service team to maintain the right tone, show empathy and provide up-to-date information, so that they do not give out conflicting information.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_16: Subject: Update on deliveries — fuel supply situation in Europe
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_2: As you are probably aware, the European fuel supply is under pressure due to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. We would like to keep you informed in good time about what this means for your deliveries.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html: From [date], delivery times may increase from 3 to 5 or 7 working days.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html_2: Fuel surcharge — from [date], we will apply a surcharge of [X%] to transport-sensitive orders, based on the current diesel price index. This surcharge is reviewed on a monthly basis.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html_3: Consolidated deliveries — to minimise the number of journeys, we group orders by region. You can specify a preferred delivery date.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_3: We are following the National Oil Crisis Plan and optimising our logistics: route planning, fleet management and alternative suppliers. Our aim is to minimise the impact on you as much as possible.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_4: You will receive an update every two weeks via [email / customer portal]. If you have any questions about an order in the meantime, please feel free to contact us on [phone number] or via [email address].
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_5: We appreciate your understanding and trust.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_20: This message combines the key elements of effective crisis communication: providing information at an early stage, being transparent, keeping things concrete, prioritising the customer’s perspective, and offering a way forward.[15][14] [17]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html_4: Reduce and optimise your vehicle fleet — consider a mobility budget instead of company cars. [12]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html_5: Fill up outside motorways, combine journeys by region and reward fuel-efficient driving. [12]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li: Carry out technical adjustments: remove roof panels, check tyre pressure, use a lower-viscosity engine oil.
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html_6: Join the large-scale consumers’ consultation group and the trade associations that are involved in discussions with the government regarding the LCP-O. [8]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.li_html_7: Monitor decisions by the EU Coordination Group and IEA market reports for early indicators.[10] [5]
editorial_oil_shortage_2026.p_html_21: The better prepared you are internally, the more credible your external communications will be.

featured.title_html: When it comes down to it, help must be able to find each other.
featured.deck: Why emergency organisations, volunteer networks and local entrepreneurs need to become more visible in Europe's resilience landscape.

editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.h2: A city becomes strong before the crisis
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_2: Sometimes the power goes out. Sometimes the internet goes down. Sometimes payments stop working. Sometimes a cyberattack hits a municipality, a hospital, a port company or an energy utility. Extreme weather, the threat of war, sabotage and disinformation can also disrupt a city.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html: Rotterdam is therefore preparing with a Resilience Programme. The municipality wants the city, its neighbourhoods and its own organisation to be better ready for major disruptions. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_3: That lesson matters not only for Rotterdam. It matters for the whole of Europe.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_4: A city does not become resilient during a crisis. A city becomes resilient in the years, months and weeks before. In everyday conversations. In good agreements. In businesses that know each other. In neighbourhoods where people know who needs help. In municipalities that keep communicating, even when systems fail.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_6: That is more than a nice line. It is a way of working.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_7: Resilience can sound like a big word. But really it is about something very practical.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_8: Can the municipality reach residents when the internet fails? Can a care provider keep running when suppliers arrive late? Can a supermarket stay open when card payments stop? Does a logistics company know whom to call when a port route closes? Does an elderly resident know where to find help?
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_9: These are not abstract questions. They decide whether a city stays calm when pressure builds.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html_2: In its programme Rotterdam names, among other things, geopolitical tensions, hybrid threats, digital attacks and the disruption of vital processes such as power, data, telephony and internet. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_10: Behind those words is ordinary life. A parent who cannot reach a child. A business owner who cannot deliver. A family doctor without access to systems. A neighbourhood team that does not know which residents need extra help.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_11: That is why crisis preparation must be understandable. Not only for specialists, but for everyone.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_12: A city is not only a town hall and a network of streets. A city is also a network of businesses.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_13: Think of transport companies, port operators, installers, supermarkets, care suppliers, construction firms, energy companies, telecom providers, security firms, food producers and small entrepreneurs in the neighbourhood.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_14: They matter not only for the economy. They matter for safety and continuity as well.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_15: When a regional business fails, it can directly affect residents. When a cold chain breaks, it affects food. When an installer is unavailable, repairs take longer. When a logistics company halts, medicines, parts or groceries arrive late. When a local IT firm is unprepared, several clients can be hit at once.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_16: Good cooperation therefore starts before the crisis.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_17: A mayor can invite businesses to the table. But businesses can also take the first step themselves. They can map their dependencies. They can practise power or internet outages. They can make agreements with suppliers and customers. They can take part in regional crisis exercises.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_18: Resilience is not only a public responsibility. It is also entrepreneurship.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.h2_5: The port makes this a European matter
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html_3: Rotterdam is a city, but it is also a European hub. The port holds a strategic position in the Netherlands and Europe. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html_4: Goods, energy, raw materials and logistics flows all pass through the port. In a serious international scenario, Rotterdam may also play a role in military transit and support for allies. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_29: That makes the city strong. But it also makes the city vulnerable.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_30: When a port, a rail line, a bridge, a data link or an energy supply is hit, it is not felt by a single municipality alone. It is felt by businesses, regions and countries.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_31: Crisis preparation is therefore not a local hobby. It is European security in practice.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_32: Every port city can learn from this. So can every border region. Every industrial region. Every city with a hospital, a data centre, an energy hub or a major logistics function.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_33: During a major disruption, help does not always arrive everywhere at the same time.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_34: The first hours are often local. Sometimes even street by street.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html_5: Rotterdam is therefore working on neighbourhood resilience and emergency hubs where residents can turn during a crisis for information, help and basic services. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_36: A crisis is not only resolved in a crisis centre. A crisis is also absorbed by neighbours, neighbourhood teams, entrepreneurs, schools, faith communities, sports clubs and local businesses.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_37: A baker with a generator can matter. A transport company with drivers can matter. A supermarket manager with local knowledge can matter. An installation firm that knows where the vulnerable buildings are can matter.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_38: In many crisis plans such people are not central. But in reality they can make the difference.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_39: Not everyone starts from the same position.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_40: Some people have money for an emergency kit. Others do not. Some people understand official websites. Others do not. Some people have family nearby. Others are alone. Some residents do not speak the language of the authorities well.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html_6: Rotterdam therefore works together with organisations such as Voedselbank Rotterdam and Stichting Salaam to make emergency kits available to people with fewer financial resources. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_42: A city is only truly resilient when vulnerable people are included too.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_43: The same applies to businesses. Large companies often have departments for risk, safety and continuity. Small businesses usually do not. Yet small entrepreneurs are essential in neighbourhoods and regional supply chains.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_44: They must be able to take part as well.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_45: That calls for simple language, practical agreements and short exercises. Not thick reports, but useful questions.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_46: What must keep working? Whom do you need? Who needs you? What do you do if power, internet or staff fail?
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_47: When municipalities help businesses with this, the whole region grows stronger.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_48: In a crisis, people look for something to hold on to.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_49: They want to know what is happening. They want to know what is safe. They want to know where help is. They want to know whether they can travel, pay, call or use water.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_html_7: Rotterdam is therefore looking at emergency communications, including through KPN's National Crisis Network and possible additions such as satellite communications and MESH networks. [1]
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_51: Communication must also be understandable. Short. Honest. Multilingual. Repeatable. And delivered by people who are trusted.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_52: A crisis message must not sound like policy jargon.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_53: Not: "There is a disruption of vital infrastructure."
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_54: But: "The power is out. Stay inside if you can. Check on your neighbours. For help, go to the emergency point at the school."
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_55: Businesses have a role here too. Employers reach staff. Shops reach customers. Care organisations reach clients. Port companies reach suppliers. Local entrepreneurs reach the neighbourhood.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_56: In a crisis, communication is not only a government task. It is a shared responsibility.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_57: A mayor does not have to wait for perfect plans.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_58: The first step is to open the conversation. With residents. With businesses. With emergency services. With schools. With healthcare. With civic organisations. With the region.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_59: Not with the question: "Who is responsible if things go wrong?"
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_60: But with the question: "What must we keep going together?"
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_61: That conversation changes the tone. It makes crisis preparation less legalistic and more human. Less distant and more practical.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_62: Mayors can bring calm by making clear now what matters. They can invite businesses in as partners. They can take neighbourhoods seriously. They can demand plain language. They can treat exercises not as a box-ticking duty but as a way to build trust.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_63: A city that practises together is less likely to panic.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_64: Regional businesses do not have to wait for a crisis letter from the municipality.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_66: Not with complex models, but with a simple conversation in the management team.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_67: Which of our services must keep running? Which customers depend on us? Which suppliers do we need ourselves? What happens if the internet is out for two days? What happens if fuel becomes scarce? What happens if staff cannot get to work? Who decides then?
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_68: After that, the question becomes regional.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_69: Which nearby businesses do we need? Which municipal contacts do we know? Which emergency services must know what we do? Can we mean something to the neighbourhood in a crisis?
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_70: For many businesses this is not a cost item. It is continuity. It is reputation. It is care for staff. It is responsibility for the region in which they work.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_71: A prepared business does not only help itself. It helps the city.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_72: Residents do not need to be afraid either.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_73: Preparing is not panic. Preparing creates calm.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_74: Know your neighbours. Think about water, light, medicines and important phone numbers. Know who on your street needs extra help. Make arrangements with your family. Follow trusted information from the municipality and emergency services.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_75: But perhaps the most important thing is simpler still: don't wait for the crisis to meet the people around you.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_76: A resilient neighbourhood starts with contact.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_77: Rotterdam shows that crisis preparation consists of three layers of mutual coordination and cooperation.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_78: The first is the strategic layer. It covers geopolitics, the port, vital infrastructure and hybrid threats.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_79: The second is the organisational layer. It covers the municipality, crisis decision-making, emergency communications, continuity and cooperation with regional businesses.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_80: The third is the human layer. It covers residents, entrepreneurs, neighbourhood networks, food banks, faith communities and trust.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_81: These three layers must reinforce each other.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_82: If the strategic layer is missing, a city sees major threats too late.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_83: If the organisational layer is missing, responsibilities remain unclear.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_84: If the human layer is missing, help does not reach the places where it is needed most.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_86: Between government and business. Between port and city. Between the crisis organisation and neighbourhood networks. Between administrators and residents. Between plans on paper and people in practice.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_87: That is why looking together at risks, responsibilities and what must remain protected matters so much — between administrators, businesses and civic partners.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_88: Europe talks a lot about security. But security also begins very close to home.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_89: With a mayor who speaks plainly. With a port company that knows its role in the chain. With a neighbourhood that knows who needs help. With a regional business that keeps delivering when things get hard. With a municipality that can communicate even without the internet.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_90: Rotterdam shows that resilience can be made practical.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_91: Not in one grand plan that solves everything. But in many small steps that reinforce each other.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_92: Street by street. Business by business. Neighbourhood by neighbourhood. City by city. Region by region.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_93: The future will not become quieter on its own.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_95: Not through fear. Not through panic. Not by writing thick reports alone.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_96: But through preparation, cooperation and trust.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_97: Rotterdam shows that a resilient city is not built by government alone. It is built by residents, businesses, civic organisations and administrators together.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_98: The most important lesson is simple:
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_99: When things go wrong, no one should be on their own.
editorial_rotterdam_weerbaarheid_2026.p_100: That is not only a message for Rotterdam.

editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p: A crisis rarely begins with an official announcement.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_2: It begins with smoke on the horizon. With water rising faster than expected. With a neighbourhood without power. With an older resident who doesn't know where to go. With a small-business owner who keeps the doors open while the surroundings come to a halt. With volunteers moving before systems can scale up.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_3: In moments like these, what resilience really means becomes clear.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_4: Not just policy. Not just plans. Not just systems. But people, organisations and businesses who can find each other when it matters.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_5: That is why emergency organisations, humanitarian initiatives, civil protection networks, search-and-rescue teams, shelter organisations, psychosocial responders, local volunteer groups and engaged entrepreneurs deserve a visible place on the European security and resilience map.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_6: Not because they are decorative. Because they are capacity.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.h2: The layer between policy and reality
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_7: Europe is increasingly talking about strategic autonomy, critical infrastructure, civil protection and societal resilience.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_8: And rightly so. Risks are no longer neatly separated. A flood becomes a logistics problem. A cyberattack becomes a public information problem. A power outage becomes a care problem. A fire, storm or evacuation turns within hours into a test of governance, trust, communication and practical help.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_html: The European Union recognises this in its civil protection architecture: the EU Civil Protection Mechanism is designed to strengthen cooperation on prevention, preparedness and response between EU countries and participating states. [1] The Emergency Response Coordination Centre acts as the 24/7 coordination hub for emergency aid, expertise, civil protection teams and specialised resources. [2]
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_9: But European coordination only works if there is local and regional capacity to plug into.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_10: That capacity does not live solely in government agencies. It lives in organisations that know who is vulnerable, where shelter is possible, which volunteers are available, which routes still work, where food or materials are stored, which language is needed, which trust already exists and which help is actually feasible today.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_11: That is the layer between policy and reality.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_12: And precisely this layer needs to become more visible.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.h2_2: Emergency is more than emergency response
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_13: The word "emergency" sounds as though it only refers to acting after something goes wrong.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_14: But the best emergency organisations are at work well before the crisis. They train. They drill. They map vulnerabilities. They build local networks. They prepare shelters. They maintain equipment. They train volunteers. They connect residents, businesses, municipalities, care providers, schools and civil-society organisations.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_html_2: The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction stresses the same logic: understand risk, strengthen risk governance, invest in resilience and improve preparedness for effective response and recovery. [3]
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_15: That sounds abstract, but in practice it is simple.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_16: Does a neighbourhood know who needs help with evacuation? Does a municipality know which organisations can mobilise volunteers within two hours? Does a business know where to turn during a prolonged power outage? Does a relief organisation know which local entrepreneurs can provide vehicles, space, cooling capacity, tools or communication channels?
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_17: Anyone who only starts asking these questions during a crisis is already too late.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_18: A crisis atlas is not an address book.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_19: A good atlas shows where capacity sits. It makes visible which organisations can contribute to emergency aid, disaster response, shelter, evacuation, food supply, psychosocial support, citizen assistance and recovery.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_20: That is why it matters that relief organisations do not disappear into categories that are too general.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_21: A food organisation that provides basic needs in a crisis is not merely "social". A volunteer group that evacuates or supports people is not merely "local". A search-and-rescue team is not merely "operational". A psychosocial relief organisation is not merely "care". A business network that makes materials, space or logistics available is not merely "business".
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_22: Within the Atlas, these organisations belong under the Emergency & Crisis Response sector, with secondary tags that clarify their function:
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_23: This is not a cosmetic taxonomy. It is a way of making help findable before help is needed.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_24: In a crisis, people do not automatically trust an unfamiliar system.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_25: They trust familiar faces. Local organisations. Volunteers who have helped before. Entrepreneurs who are present in the neighbourhood. Responders who speak the language. Communities that know who is reliable. Networks that already existed before things went wrong.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_27: It means that warnings are followed sooner. That vulnerable people are reached earlier. That information is more credible. That panic gets less room. That formal agencies better understand what is really happening on the ground.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_28: Emergency organisations are therefore not only operators. They are translators.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_29: They translate policy into help. They translate risk into action. They translate uncertainty into something to hold on to.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_30: That is exactly why they must be known, visible and connected.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_31: Crisis preparedness is not only a task for government, emergency services and civil-society organisations. Small and medium-sized businesses also carry a quiet but essential responsibility.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_32: Entrepreneurs know how dependent a community is on practical continuity: a baker who stays open, a transporter who can still deliver, an installer who fixes outages, a shopkeeper who knows people, a local company that can make space, materials, vehicles, knowledge or staff available when needed.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_33: In a crisis, entrepreneurship becomes more than economic activity. It becomes societal capacity.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_34: That is why it is important that entrepreneurs support and strengthen one another in practical ways. Not out of abstract ideals, but out of sober responsibility: knowing who can do what, which resources are locally available, how to reach each other, which vulnerabilities exist and how together to prevent a disruption from growing larger than it needs to.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_35: This requires no grand words. It requires willingness.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_36: Willingness to look ahead. To see neighbours, suppliers, customers and local organisations not only as market parties, but as part of the same resilient network. To make arrangements in calm times that make a difference in hard times.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_37: For small and medium-sized businesses there is also a moral assignment here. Those who run a business also build trust. Those who provide employment, services, logistics, food, technology, care, communication or local presence contribute to the stability of a community.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_38: Crisis resilience does not only emerge in command centres. It emerges in workshops, warehouses, shops, offices, family firms, cooperatives and local business networks.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_39: So the question is not only: how do we protect our own business?
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_40: The larger question is: how do we ensure that our business, when it matters, also helps others stay on their feet?
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_41: That is not charity. That is mature entrepreneurship.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_42: And it is exactly where practical cooperation and moral conviction meet: entrepreneurs who strengthen one another also strengthen the society in which they operate.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_43: Resilience is often discussed in technical terms.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_44: Capacity. Governance. Response structures. Continuity planning. Critical processes. Risk analysis.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_45: That is necessary, but not enough.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_46: Under every resilient society lies a moral choice: do we let people and organisations face disruption in isolation, or do we build connections through which help becomes available faster?
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_47: Emergency organisations show that practical support and moral conviction are not opposites. They belong together.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_48: Those who organise emergency aid are effectively saying: no one should have to face it alone when things go wrong. Those who train volunteers say: responsibility can be shared. Those who prepare shelters say: dignity still matters under pressure. Those who involve local entrepreneurs say: economy and community are not separate worlds.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_49: That is the core of public value.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_50: Not everything has to be centrally steered. Not everything has to be large. But whatever is locally, regionally and European-level available must be visible, reliable and connected.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_51: Europe must not only protect its infrastructure.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_52: It must also protect the people and organisations that keep society functioning during disruption.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_53: That means: taking volunteer capacity seriously. Better connecting humanitarian networks. Making local knowledge visible. Involving entrepreneurs in preparation. Not treating civil protection as separate from community. And not calling emergency organisations only when it is already too late.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_html_3: The EU Civil Protection Mechanism allows countries to ask for help during disasters and allows Member States or participating states to offer resources, teams and expertise. [1] But the quality of response is also shaped by the capacity that was already known, exercised and connected before the crisis.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_54: That is why better crisis response does not start with the siren.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_55: It starts with the map. With the relationship. With the agreement. With the question: who can help, with what, where, and when?
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_56: For ESRF.net the task is clear.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_57: The Atlas should not only show formal institutions. It should also make visible the practical help structure of Europe.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_58: That is why emergency organisations belong recognisably under Emergency & Crisis Response. That is why secondary tags matter. That is why humanitarian aid, disaster relief, civil protection, search & rescue, shelter, food, volunteer response, psychosocial support, community resilience and crisis response must each be findable.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_59: And that is why small and medium-sized businesses belong in this conversation too.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_60: Because a resilient Europe is not only built by governments, funds or large institutions. It is built by thousands of organisations, entrepreneurs and communities willing to support each other in practical ways.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_61: Not out of fear. Not out of posturing. But out of responsibility.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_62: When it comes down to it, help must be able to find each other.
editorial_emergency_capaciteit_europa_20.p_63: That is the minimum promise of a resilient society.

editorial_koningsdag_2026.h2: A day that turns the country orange
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p: Today the Netherlands celebrates the birthday of King Willem-Alexander.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_2: And with that birthday the whole country celebrates something larger.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_3: Cities turn orange. Villages turn orange. Gardens, balconies, schoolyards, market streets and canals turn orange. Children sell toys on a blanket. Music plays from speakers and brass bands. Flea markets fill up. Small boats drift through the city. Neighbours speak to neighbours. Strangers smile at each other.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_4: It is a celebration. A real celebration.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_5: But beneath that celebration lies something quieter. Something we do not always say out loud, but feel all the same.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_6: We celebrate that we can celebrate this.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.h2_2: A birthday that binds us together
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_7: The birthday of a King is more than the birthday of one person.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_8: It is a day on which the whole country gathers around a single moment. Three generations walk down the same street. People of different backgrounds, professions, opinions and beliefs stand side by side in the same colour. A mayor walks among the people, not behind a fence. A child plays safely in the street. A family drifts past the canal and waves to strangers.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_10: It is something generations before us built up, protected and passed on. Freedom in its everyday form. Safety we do not have to explain, because it simply works.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_11: On this day, the King's birthday, the Netherlands makes visible what it carries the rest of the year: a society that, despite everything, finds its way back to one another.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.h2_3: A gift for all of us
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_12: A birthday is normally about the person whose birthday it is. On King's Day it is also about us.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_13: Because this day is, quietly, a gift for all of us — to one another, to the generations before and after us, to everyone who helps build a country that may be like this. It is a gift we do not only receive, but also give. By seeking out our neighbours. By laughing together. By acknowledging, just for a moment, that it is no small thing that we stand here, today, together in peace and security.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_14: In a more restless world, that is something to pause for. Not out of fear. Out of gratitude.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.h2_4: A wish for the whole of Europe
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_15: What the Netherlands feels on this day, we wish for every country in Europe.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_16: A day on which a country comes together. A day on which differences may step into the background for a while, without disappearing. A day on which children play safely, elders look on at ease, and leaders walk among their people without fear.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_17: Europe is a continent of languages, histories and rediscoveries. But Europe is also a continent of squares, markets and street parties. Each country has its own King's Day in another form.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_18: What binds us is the longing to live together in peace and security.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_19: That longing is not weakness. It is a duty.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_20: Today is a day to be outside. To hear music. To buy something from a blanket on the pavement. To compliment a stranger on his orange hat. To meet neighbours you did not yet know.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_21: But somewhere beneath all that orange, a quieter thought may walk along.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_22: That on the birthday of our King we celebrate together that we may live in peace and security. That this does not happen by itself. That it is carried by people we often do not see. And that we wish this day, with all our heart, to everyone on this continent.
editorial_koningsdag_2026.p_24: For the Netherlands. And for Europe.

fund.hero_deck: ESRF.net huwa sostnut mill-komunità Ewropea li jservi. Mhux minn ftit donaturi kbar, iżda mis-saħħa kollettiva ta' ħafna kontribuzzjonijiet żgħar. Donazzjonijiet jiġu aċċettati biss minn organizzazzjonijiet u individwi fl-Ewropa u minn membri tan-network ESRF.net. Hija għażla deliberata — l-indipendenza tibda minn kif tkun iffinanzjat.
fund.idea_sub: Ħafna donazzjonijiet żgħar, network reżiljenti wieħed.
fund.idea_body_1_html: Stichting ESRF European Security and Resilience Fund hija fondazzjoni mingħajr skop ta' qligħ. Ma tbigħx listings, ma tiġborx miżati ta' sħubija, u ma taċċettax classifications imħallsa. Il-katalogu huwa — u jibqa' — ġid pubbliku.
fund.idea_body_2: Biex ikopri l-ispejjeż operattivi, ESRF.net huwa ffinanzjat permezz ta' tliet kanali: reklamar minimu u mhux invażiv (permezz ta' Google AdSense), donazzjonijiet volontarji mill-komunità Ewropea, u sponsorship korporattiv. Id-dħul mir-reklamar isostni l-bażi; donazzjonijiet u sponsorships jiffinanzjaw it-tkabbir u l-indipendenza editorjali tan-network.
fund.idea_body_2b: Il-fondazzjoni hija mibnija fuq prinċipju wieħed: ħafna kontribuzzjonijiet żgħar huma aktar b'saħħithom minn ftit kbar. Meta mijiet ta' organizzazzjonijiet u individwi jagħtu kull wieħed somma modesta, in-network isir awtosuffiċjenti, indipendenti, u responsabbli quddiem il-komunità — mhux quddiem benefattur wieħed. Dan il-mudell ta' finanzjament distribwit jirrifletti r-reżiljenza distribwita li l-qafas innifsu jippromwovi. Mhix biss strateġija ta' finanzjament; hija filosofija fil-prattika.
fund.idea_pull_html: Donazzjoni waħda hija ġest. Elf donazzjoni huma forza imbattibbli.
fund.idea_eligibility_html: Kontribuzzjonijiet Ewropej biss. Biex tiġi ppreservata l-indipendenza u n-natura Ewropea tal-qafas, il-fondazzjoni taċċetta donazzjonijiet biss minn organizzazzjonijiet u individwi bbażati fl-Ewropa u minn membri tan-network ESRF.net.
fund.idea_body_3: Kull ewro mogħti jmur biex iżomm l-atlas, jikkura l-katalogu, jippubblika d-Dispatch, u jespandi n-network f'{countries} pajjiżi Ewropej. Ebda spejjeż moħbija — it-trasparenza hija l-unika politika.
fund.where_intro: Kull kontribuzzjoni hija mqassma fost erba' pilastri ta' attività. Ebda fond ma jmur lill-azzjonisti — m'hemmx.
fund.alloc1_body: Xogħol kkurat editorjalment, verifika ta' listings, kwalità tad-data, u infrastruttura tal-mappa interattiva.
fund.alloc2_body: Servers, infrastruttura multilingwi għal 27 lingwa, sigurtà, u żvilupp kontinwu tal-pjattaforma.
fund.alloc3_body: Pubblikazzjoni tan-newsletter dwar is-sigurtà u r-reżiljenza, analiżi pajjiż b'pajjiż, u komunikazzjoni tan-network.
fund.alloc4_body: Amministrazzjoni tal-fondazzjoni, konformità, rappurtar finanzjarju, u rapport annwali ta' trasparenza.
fund.ways_sub: Finanzjarjament, prattikament, jew sempliċement billi xxerred il-kelma.
fund.way1_body: Kontribuzzjoni waħda ta' kwalunkwe daqs. Sew jekk hi 5 € jew 500 €, kull donazzjoni hija reġistrata b'mod trasparenti u tiġi diretta direttament lejn ix-xogħol tan-network. Ebda ammont mhu żgħir wisq.
fund.way2_body: Donazzjoni rikorrenti modesta — anki 2 € fix-xahar — tagħti lill-fondazzjoni xi ħaġa aktar prezzjuża minn ammont kbir wieħed: prevedibbiltà. Dħul prevedibbli jippermetti ppjanar aħjar: aktar lingwi, aktar listings ivverifikati, aktar pajjiżi. Komunità li tappoġġja b'mod konsistenti tibni aktar minn waħda li tiddependi fuq każwalità.
fund.way3_body_html: Organizzazzjonijiet li jfittxu sħubija aktar profonda jistgħu jikkunsidraw il-livelli tas-sponsorship tagħna — Founding Patron, Programme Partner, jew Community Supporter. Is-sponsorships huma żvelati b'mod trasparenti u qatt ma jinfluwenzaw deċiżjonijiet editorjali.
fund.way4_body: Mhux il-kontribuzzjonijiet kollha huma monetarji. Għajnuna fit-traduzzjoni, verifika tad-data, kontribuzzjoni editorjali, jew għarfien tekniku — il-ħiliet tal-komunità huma l-akbar assi tan-network. Ikkuntattjana jekk tixtieq tagħti ħin jew kompetenza.
fund.way5_body: Aqsam ESRF.net mal-kollegi, mas-sħab, u man-networks. Kull listing ġdid, kull qarrej ġdid, kull artiklu maqsum isaħħaħ il-komunità. Il-viżibbiltà hija forma ta' kontribuzzjoni.
fund.impact1: Tkopri l-hosting ta' paġna ta' pajjiż wieħed għal xahar.
fund.impact2: Tiffinanzja r-reviżjoni editorjali u l-verifika ta' ħames listings ġodda.
fund.impact3: Issostni edizzjoni waħda tan-newsletter Dispatch — riċerka, edituri, u tixrid.
fund.impact4: Tippermetti li l-pjattaforma kollha tiġi tradotta f'lingwa Ewropea oħra.
fund.transparency_sub: Kull ewro magħdud, kull deċiżjoni żvelata.
fund.transparency_body_1: Stichting ESRF European Security and Resilience Fund jippubblika rapport annwali ta' trasparenza b'kull dħul, spiża, u deċiżjoni ta' allokazzjoni. Il-fondazzjoni hija reġistrata fl-Olanda u soġġetta għal-liġi Olandiża dwar il-fondazzjonijiet.
fund.transparency_body_2: Ebda donatur individwali — irrispettivament mill-ġenerożità — ma jirċievi influwenza editorjali. Is-separazzjoni bejn il-finanzjament u l-editorjali hija assoluta: id-donaturi jiffinanzjaw l-infrastruttura, l-editorjali jħares l-indipendenza. Mhix politika; hija r-raġuni għaliex jeżisti n-network.
fund.transparency_item1: Rapport finanzjarju annwali ppubblikat fuq dan is-sit
fund.transparency_item2: L-isponsors kollha 'l fuq minn 5,000 € fuq lista pubblika
fund.transparency_item3: Ebda listings sponsorjati, ebda classifications imħallsa, ebda obbligi moħbija
fund.transparency_item4: Separazzjoni sħiħa ta' finanzjament u deċiżjonijiet editorjali
fund.transparency_item5: Reklamar minimu u mhux invażiv (Google AdSense) biex iżomm il-bażi ssir
fund.transparency_item6: Donazzjonijiet aċċettati biss minn sorsi Ewropej u membri tan-network ESRF.net
fund.cta_body_html: Donazzjonijiet huma proċessati permezz ta' trasferiment SEPA lill-kont bankarju tal-fondazzjoni. Għal donazzjonijiet rikorrenti jew mistoqsijiet, jekk jogħġbok ikkuntattja direttament.
fund.cta_eligibility_html: Eliġibbiltà: Il-fondazzjoni taċċetta donazzjonijiet biss minn organizzazzjonijiet u individwi bbażati fl-Ewropa u minn membri tan-network ESRF.net. Kontribuzzjonijiet barra dan il-grupp jiġu rritornati b'mod ġentili.
fund.cta_contact_html: Mistoqsijiet jew mod ieħor kif tikkontribwixxi? Ibgħat email lil hello@esrf.net.
fund.faq1_a: ESRF.net jaċċetta donazzjonijiet biss minn organizzazzjonijiet u individwi bbażati fl-Ewropa u minn membri tan-network ESRF.net, irrispettivament mil-lokazzjoni. Din il-politika tippreżerva n-natura Ewropea u l-indipendenza tal-qafas. Kontribuzzjonijiet barra dan il-grupp jiġu rritornati b'mod ġentili.
fund.faq2_a: Stichting ESRF European Security and Resilience Fund hija fondazzjoni Olandiża mingħajr skop ta' qligħ. Id-deduċibbiltà tiddependi mir-residenza tiegħek u mil-liġijiet tat-taxxa lokali. Nirrakkomandaw li tikkonsulta konsulent tat-taxxa. Il-fondazzjoni toħroġ irċevuti fuq talba.
fund.faq3_a: Iva. Donazzjonijiet anonimi jiġu aċċettati u rispettati. Fir-rapport annwali ta' trasparenza, il-fondazzjoni tippubblika t-total ta' kontribuzzjonijiet anonimi mingħajr ma tismi donaturi individwali. Jekk tippreferi li ismek ikun viżibbli, sempliċement għidilna.
fund.faq4_a: Assolutament le. Is-separazzjoni bejn il-finanzjament u l-editorjali hija mingħajr kundizzjonijiet. Donazzjonijiet — irrispettivament mid-daqs — m'għandhom ebda effett fuq liema organizzazzjonijiet huma listjati, kif huma deskritti, jew x'jagħżel li jkopri d-Dispatch. Din l-indipendenza mhix negozjabbli.
fund.faq5_q: X'inhi d-differenza bejn donazzjoni u sponsorship?
fund.faq5_a_html: Donazzjoni hija kontribuzzjoni mingħajr kundizzjonijiet — kwalunkwe ammont, ebda aspettattiva ta' viżibbiltà. Sponsorship hija sħubija strutturata b'livelli definiti, viżibbiltà tal-marka, u ftehim annwali. It-tnejn jiġu żvelati fir-rapport ta' trasparenza; l-ebda waħda ma tagħti influwenza editorjali.
fund.faq6_q: Kemm-il darba jiġi ppubblikat ir-rapport ta' trasparenza?
fund.faq6_a: Annwalment, wara l-għeluq tas-sena finanzjarja. Ir-rapport huwa ppubblikat fuq dan is-sit u jinkludi d-dħul totali, l-analiżi tan-nefqa, lista tas-sponsors żvelati, u deskrizzjoni ta' kif il-fondi ntużaw biex javvanzaw in-network.
